mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.5 percent, consumer spending growth stagnates and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 7.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.01 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month through improved earnings expectations.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of triggered cases, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive proxies with p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals strengthening economic momentum that triggers short-term momentum buying in equities, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during expansion phases.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative 5-day returns in >55% of observations during expansion regimes, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate profit pools and dividend capacity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings revisions follow macro data.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in >60% of periods following positive GDPC1 revisions, or the correlation between GDPC1 growth and SPX returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 235.0, inflation expectations rise and force the Federal Reserve to consider tightening policy, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of observations when CPIAUCSL crosses the threshold, or the signal fails to predict returns after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment indicates a healthy labor market absorption rate, which boosts consumer spending forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during late-cycle expansions signals capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in >55% of late-cycle observations, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS trajectory.
